skill.name: css-development-validate
skill.description: Reviews existing CSS for naming consistency, Tailwind composition, dark mode coverage, tests, documentation, and reusable style patterns.
skill.summary: Validate CSS against semantic naming, Tailwind, dark mode, testing, and documentation patterns.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown backticks, prose, and CSS example snippets. No executable scripts, network behavior, prompt injection, credential access, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with a low risk note for false-positive static detections.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code spans and fenced examples in SKILL.md. They describe CSS review steps and sample CSS, HTML, TypeScript, and Markdown output rather than Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown formatting and example blocks only. I found no executable script, command string construction, or instruction to run external commands.

low_findings.title: False positive: Prose flagged as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections occur in ordinary descriptive text and class naming examples. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, password handling, hashing calls, MD5, DES, SHA1, or encryption code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is CSS review documentation, not security-sensitive code. The static matches appear to come from substrings in words such as description or examples.

low_findings.title: False positive: CSS review text flagged as network reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detections are inside a sample HTML button and prose about repeated style patterns. No evidence found of network scanning, host discovery, sockets, HTTP requests, or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain CSS validation examples, not networking APIs or reconnaissance tooling. The surrounding workflow remains limited to reading and reviewing project CSS files.

content.value_statement: CSS changes can drift from naming, dark mode, and test standards. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured stylesheet review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews CSS class names for semantic and descriptive naming.
Checks whether Tailwind utilities are composed with @apply.
Identifies missing dark mode variants on colored and interactive styles.
Looks for repeated style patterns that can be extracted into base classes.
Checks for static CSS tests and component rendering tests.
Produces a structured CSS validation report with file and line references.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable validation scripts.
It depends on the assistant reading the relevant project files correctly.
It focuses on CSS architecture patterns, not full accessibility testing.
It assumes Tailwind and semantic component class patterns are desired.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge stylesheet review
use_cases.description: Review changed CSS before merging to catch naming, dark mode, and test coverage issues.

use_cases.title: Design system consistency audit
use_cases.description: Check whether component classes follow shared naming and composition patterns across a project.

use_cases.title: Refactor planning for CSS debt
use_cases.description: Identify repeated styles, missing documentation, and classes that should be renamed before a cleanup pass.

prompt_templates.title: Review one stylesheet
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single CSS file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CSS file for semantic naming, Tailwind @apply usage, dark mode coverage, tests, and documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit component styles
prompt_templates.scenario: Review CSS together with related components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these component styles and related component files. Report naming issues, repeated utilities, missing dark mode variants, and missing tests.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for refactoring
prompt_templates.scenario: Find improvement areas before changing CSS structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the current CSS and identify refactor opportunities. Prioritize repeated patterns, unclear class names, and missing coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Gate a pull request
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a merge decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CSS change as a pull request gate. Provide pass or fail guidance with file references, severity, and recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: A stylesheet contains utility-based class names and several colored components without dark mode variants.
output_examples.output: The report lists semantic naming issues, identifies missing dark mode coverage, and recommends clearer component class names.

output_examples.input: A component library repeats similar card styles across multiple classes.
output_examples.output: The report identifies the repeated pattern, suggests a shared base class, and explains the expected maintenance benefit.

output_examples.input: A new CSS component was added without tests or usage comments.
output_examples.output: The report flags missing static and rendering tests, then recommends documentation comments for the new class.

best_practices: Review CSS with the related component files so class usage is clear.
Ask for file and line references for every reported issue.
Prioritize dark mode and test gaps before documentation cleanup.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for visual regression testing.
Do not apply Tailwind @apply guidance to projects that intentionally avoid Tailwind.
Do not rename classes without checking component usage and tests.

faq.question: Does this skill modify CSS files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides review and reporting. The user can request fixes after the report.

faq.question: Can it review Tailwind projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It specifically checks whether Tailwind utilities are composed into semantic classes with @apply.

faq.question: Does it require a specific test framework?
faq.answer: No. It looks for static CSS tests and component rendering tests in the project structure.

faq.question: Can it find dark mode problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks colored and interactive styles for missing dark mode variants.

faq.question: Is this a security scanning skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a frontend CSS quality review skill, not a vulnerability scanner.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before merging CSS changes, during design system audits, or before planning a CSS refactor.
